content.full_text:
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 ••• 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814 
(916) 322-5662 
Conway Collis 
Board of Equalization 
7859 Fairchild 
Canoga Park, CA 
91306 
Administration 
322-5660 
Executive/legal 
322-5901 
April 29, 1983 
Enforcement 
322-6441 
Statements of Economic Interest 
322-6444 
Attention: 
Gretchen Parker 
Re: 
AdvIce Letter No. A-83-109 
Dear Mr. Collis: 
We have received your amendments to your 1982 Statement of 
Economic Interests ("SEl"). 
prior to amending your SEl, you 
asked Alice Hugnes whether certain loans used in your campaign 
had to be disclosed on your SEl. 
Although you have already 
amended your SEI for 1982, you have asked us to advise you as to 
the proper method of reporting such loans in the future. 
All loans used by you or your campaign for political 
purposes must be reported on your campaign statement or your 
committee's campaign statement. 
Section 84216.l1 
Therefore, 
any loan made to you personally must be reported on a campaign 
statement to the extent that you use proceeds of the loan for 
your campaign or you turn the proceeds of the loan over to your 
campaign committee.~1 Of course, any loan made directly to 
your campaign committee must be reported on the committee's 
campaign statement. 
!I All statutory references are to tne Government Code 
unless otherwise noted. 
~I In such a situation, the person who loans you the 
money is listed as the lender on the campaign statement. 


Conway Collis 
April 29, 1983 
page 2 
In addition to the reporting required on the campaign 
statements, certain loans used for political purposes must be 
disclosed on your SEl. 
Whether or not a loan used for political 
purposes must be disclosed on your SEI depends on whether or not 
the loan is a 
"contr~bution" within the meaning of the Political 
Reform Act. 
A loan which is a contribution does not have to be disclosed 
on your SEI if it is reported on your campaign statement. 
Section 82030(b) (1) specifically excludes such a contribution 
from the definition of income. 
However, not all loans which are 
used for political purposes by you or by your campaign are 
contributions. 
A loan which is not a contribution has to be 
reported on your SEl, since such a loan is income within the 
meaning of the Act. 
Section 82030. 
Section 84216 sets forth the criteria for determining 
whether or not a loan is a contribution: 
Notwithstanding Section 82015, a loan received by 
a candidate or committee is a contribution unless the 
loan is received from a commercial lending institution 
in the ordinary course of business or it is clear from 
the surrounding circumstances that it is not made for 
political purposes. 
Therefore, if you receive a loan from a commercial lending 
institution in the ordinary course of business, and use the 
proceeds for political purposes, the loan is not a 
contribution. 
The loan must be disclosed on your SEI. 
A loan 
from a commercial lending institution made in the ordinary 
course of business directly to your campaign will also be 
considered income to you, and must be disclosed on your SEl, if 
you are personally liable for the loan. 
A loan is considered to 
have been made in the ordinary course of business if it is made 
on terms generally available to the puolic, i.e., on the same 
terms, with the same security, and with the same interest rate 
as would be required for any member of the public without regard 
to official status. 
Any loan used for political purposes which is not made by a 
commercial lending institution in the ordinary course of 
business is considered a contribution unless it is clear from 
the surrounding circumstances that the lender did not make the 
loan for political purposes. 
A loan which is a contribution 
does not nave to be reported on your SEI. 
If you have a 


Conway Collis 
April 29, 1983 
Page 3 
question as to whether any particular loan is or is not a 
contribution, please feel free to contact us for more specific 
advice. 
BAM: krn 
Very truly yours, 
A 
• 
,j ~ 
t ~ 
,~"cl\ 
\" 
I 
r 
J : rj·l~·~ 
Barbara A. 
M~lman 
Cpief, Legal Division 
V\.,./(,./V

sections.question:
as to whether any particular loan is or is not a 
contribution, please feel free to contact us for more specific 
advice. 
BAM: krn
sections.conclusion: All loans used for political purposes must be reported on campaign statements. Loans that are 'contributions' under the Political Reform Act do not need to be disclosed on the SEI if reported on campaign statements. Loans from commercial lending institutions in ordinary course of business must be disclosed on the SEI as income. Other non-contribution loans used for political purposes must also be disclosed on the SEI. The determination depends on whether the loan meets the definition of 'contribution' under Government Code Section 84216.
sections.question_synthetic: The requestor asks whether personal and campaign loans must be reported on his Statement of Economic Interests and how such loans should be properly reported going forward.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Loans used for political purposes must be reported on campaign statements. Loans qualifying as 'contributions' under Section 84216 need not be reported on the SEI if properly reported on campaign statements, but loans from commercial lending institutions in the ordinary course of business and non-contribution loans must be disclosed on the SEI as income.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: OCR document with clear question and conclusion sections. Applicant had already amended prior SEI. Letter references Government Code Section 84216 and related provisions. Contains footnotes clarifying statutory references and lender reporting.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: as to whether any particular loan is or is not a 
contribution, please feel free to contact us for more specific 
advice. 
BAM: krn

CONCLUSION: All loans used for political purposes must be reported on campaign statements. Loans that are 'contributions' under the Political Reform Act do not need to be disclosed on the SEI if reported on campaign statements. Loans from commercial lending institutions in ordinary course of business must be disclosed on the SEI as income. Other non-contribution loans used for political purposes must also be disclosed on the SEI. The determination depends on whether the loan meets the definition of 'contribution' under Government Code Section 84216.

embedding.summary: This FPPC advice letter addresses the reporting requirements for campaign loans on both campaign statements and Statements of Economic Interests, distinguishing between loans that qualify as 'contributions' versus regular income based on whether they come from commercial lending institutions in the ordinary course of business.
